one_time_description: Contribution from Treaty Land Entitlement (TLE) of $3,495,172 recorded as other item below surplus line
economic_notes: First Nation operates Last Oak Golf & Country Club, Cowessess Mall, Crooked Lake Homes Ltd., Crooked Lake Homes LP, 4C Farms Limited Partnership, Chief Red Bear Children's Lodge (family and child services), and Cowessess Ventures LP (wind/urban/energy developments). Cowessess Ventures LP had revenues of $9,157,100 and net income of $1,937,039 for year ended November 30, 2022.
notes: Fiscal year ended March 31, 2023 — statements signed October 29, 2024 (over 18 months late). Nation is in Saskatchewan. Consolidates 11 entities including Chief Red Bear Children's Lodge (family/child services funded by federal government with $18.76M restricted cash). Two GBEs accounted for by modified equity method: Cowessess Ventures LP and Cowessess Ventures Ltd. TLE contribution of $3,495,172 recorded below the surplus line as 'other item'. New asset retirement obligation of $1,180,678 recognized in current year. Large deferred revenue balance of $27.4M primarily relates to capital project and CRBCL restricted funding. FSIN revenue is negative due to $1,800,000 net deferred. Restricted cash of $32.78M includes $18.76M for Chief Red Bear Children's Lodge and $3.92M for ISC capital projects. Net financial assets per balance sheet is $27,190,364 but statement of changes shows $28,336,653 — difference may relate to budget vs actual presentation; balance sheet figure used.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
